search_queries_used: site:asahq.org preanesthesia previous anesthesia complications history
site:rcoa.ac.uk previous anesthesia records alerts preoperative assessment
site:doh.gov.ae prior anesthesia complication preoperative history
candidate_sources_rejected: single-complication guidelines applied to all prior events
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Each prior event requires its own condition-specific evidence and record review.
A patient report cannot be converted into a confirmed complication automatically.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Patients with a reported or recorded previous anesthesia-related event undergoing new preanesthesia assessment.
setting_applicability: Preanesthesia history and record review only.
UAE_applicability: International ASA standards require UAE record access, incident classification, consent, anesthesia, specialist, and facility-policy adaptation.
recency_verification: ASA standard last affirmed 2020 and awareness advisory from 2006 were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer ASA basic preanesthesia standard or awareness advisory was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The ASA standard supports documenting previous anesthesia experiences and relevant medical therapy and physical condition without inferring that a prior event was an anesthesia complication.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports previous-anesthesia history, medical therapy, focused examination, airway and physical-condition assessment, and factors affecting perioperative risk.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The awareness-specific section was reviewed but not mapped because the broad history workflow does not establish awareness as the prior event.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of records, interview about previous anesthesia experiences including prior awareness, focused risk assessment, and informing selected higher-risk patients without predicting awareness.

unresolved_source_gaps: Event type, date, procedure, technique, medicines, airway, hemodynamics, symptoms, severity, records, confirmed diagnosis, treatment, recovery, sequelae, family history, specialist advice, future plan, and patient communication remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
